violations.description: Failure to provide required notice of intent to dispose of collateral
violations.legal_basis: UCC Article 9-614 requires secured party to send debtor notice of disposition of collateral. Client claims no notice received.
violations.notes: Missing evidence weakens claim. Need confirmation client did not receive notice.

violations.description: Failure to provide notice of private sale
violations.legal_basis: UCC Article 9-613 requires notice of time and place of private disposition. No evidence notice was provided.
violations.notes: Private sale without proper notice may violate commercially reasonable standard.

violations.description: Potential failure to conduct commercially reasonable sale
violations.legal_basis: UCC Article 9-610 requires disposition be commercially reasonable. Sale amount ($18,500) may be below market value for 2020 Honda Accord.
violations.potential_remedy: Deficiency reduction based on fair market value
violations.notes: Need vehicle appraisal and sale documentation to substantiate claim.

violations.legal_basis: Deficiency notice claims $12,800 but calculation shows $10,800. $2,000 unexplained discrepancy violates duty to provide accurate accounting under UCC 9-616.
violations.notes: Strong evidence from deficiency notice itself. UACC must explain discrepancy.

violations.description: Inaccurate credit reporting of deficiency balance
violations.legal_basis: FCRA Section 623(a) requires furnishers to report accurate information. UACC reported $12,800 deficiency despite calculation showing $10,800.
violations.potential_remedy: Credit report correction, statutory damages, actual damages
violations.notes: Deficiency discrepancy directly impacts credit reporting accuracy.

violations.description: Potential failure to properly investigate credit report dispute
violations.legal_basis: If client disputes credit reporting and UACC fails to conduct reasonable investigation, FCRA Section 623(b) violation may exist.
violations.potential_remedy: Statutory damages, actual damages, attorney fees
violations.notes: Requires client to file dispute and UACC to fail investigation. Not yet applicable.
